questions.prompt: Your assistant retrieves from 8 sources for every query. Per-query latency is 4-6 seconds; cost is high. Best fix?

choices.text: Add source routing — most queries don't need all 8 sources. Rule-based or LLM-classifier picks the 2-3 sources likely relevant to the current message. Latency drops to 1-2s; cost drops 50-70%; quality holds because irrelevant context wasn't helping anyway.

questions.explanation: Multi-source retrieval scales badly without routing. A small classifier (or rule-based heuristics) picks relevant sources per query. Calendar queries don't need Slack history; deal-context queries don't need user profile. Route, then retrieve, then rerank, then inject.

questions.prompt: What's a critical privacy pattern for assistant retrieval?

choices.text: Permission-aware filtering: cross-reference current user permissions before returning context. A user moved off a Slack channel? Don't return cached context from that channel. Per-user scoping is the baseline; permission-aware filtering catches the case where a user's access has changed since indexing.

questions.explanation: User-scoped retrieval is the floor. Permission-aware retrieval (validating current access at query time, not just at index time) catches the harder case where access changed after indexing. Test for both — cross-user leaks AND cross-permission leaks within a user.
